title: Follow the boosted lane
question: If bonus_lane is 2, which lane does this program target?
boardHint: Lane numbers are simple: 1 = left, 2 = center, 3 = right.
unlockConnection: A variable can copy the visible bonus_lane value and reuse it for aiming.
successMessage: Correct. target becomes 2, so the ball is aimed at the center lane.
failureMessage: target copies bonus_lane. If bonus_lane is 2, the program aims at the center lane.

title: Choose the center lane
question: Which line should replace ??? so the program always aims at the center lane?
boardHint: The center lane uses lane number 2.
unlockConnection: Variables let you store a lane number first and then pass it into set_aim(...).
successMessage: Correct. target = 2 stores the center-lane number before set_aim(...) uses it.
failureMessage: If you always want the center lane, target needs the lane number 2.

title: Repair the aim value
question: Which replacement fixes the program so it follows the current boosted lane safely?
boardHint: set_aim(...) must stay between lane 1 and lane 3.
unlockConnection: This repair checks whether you understand that bonus_lane already contains the lane number you need.
successMessage: Correct. bonus_lane already has the current lane number, so the program can use it directly.
failureMessage: bonus_lane already stores the current lane number. Adding to it can push the aim outside the allowed range.

title: Branch to the jackpot side
question: If jackpot_side is 3, which lane is chosen?
boardHint: jackpot_side shows which side of the machine is rewarding the next branch decision.
unlockConnection: Conditionals let the machine react to live board state instead of reusing one fixed aim forever.
successMessage: Correct. The else-branch runs, so the machine aims right.
failureMessage: jackpot_side is not 1 here, so the program uses the else-branch and aims right.

title: Choose the correct condition
question: Which condition should open the right-side branch when the jackpot is on the right?
boardHint: The right side uses lane number 3 in this machine.
unlockConnection: Choosing the correct condition is how the new gate module becomes useful instead of decorative.
successMessage: Correct. jackpot_side == 3 sends the machine to the right branch at the right moment.
failureMessage: The condition has to read the jackpot side directly. Right-side jackpot means value 3.

title: Repair the side-gate logic
question: Which replacement fixes the broken branch so the program follows jackpot_side?
boardHint: A good repair should use jackpot_side and give the two branches different aims.
unlockConnection: Repair tasks check whether the new branch logic actually matches the machine state it is supposed to control.
successMessage: Correct. The repaired version reads jackpot_side and sends left vs. right to different branches.
failureMessage: The fix must actually branch on jackpot_side and choose a different lane in the else-branch.

title: What does the helper call do?
question: What happens when the main program runs follow_bonus()?
boardHint: The helper editor stores one named tactic, and the main editor can call it.
unlockConnection: Functions matter here because one reusable tactic can now control the helper system.
successMessage: Correct. Calling follow_bonus() runs that helper function once.
failureMessage: A helper call executes the helper body once at the call site.

title: Choose a reusable helper
question: Which helper body best stores a reusable 'follow the current bonus lane' function?
boardHint: The helper should contain the tactic you want to reuse, not just one unrelated line.
unlockConnection: A good helper turns repeated board logic into one named tactic.
successMessage: Correct. That helper stores the full reusable tactic: aim, then drop.
failureMessage: The helper should capture the actual repeated tactic, not just a fragment of it.

title: Repair the main-program call
question: Which line should replace ??? so the main program actually uses the helper function?
boardHint: The main editor still needs a function call to trigger the stored helper body.
unlockConnection: The helper system only matters if the main program truly calls the helper instead of skipping it.
successMessage: Correct. follow_bonus() is the line that triggers the stored helper function.
failureMessage: To use a helper, the main program needs the helper name followed by parentheses.

title: Repeated helper calls
question: How many times does the helper run here?
boardHint: Everything inside the loop body repeats once per range value.
unlockConnection: Loops let one good helper function feed the machine several times in the same run.
successMessage: Correct. range(3) repeats the helper call three times.
failureMessage: The loop body runs once for each value in range(3), so the helper runs three times.

title: Choose the burst size
question: Which range value should replace ? if you want four repeated drops into the machine?
boardHint: The number inside range(...) decides how many times the tactic repeats.
unlockConnection: Choosing the correct range is how a loop becomes a deliberate burst pattern instead of random repetition.
successMessage: Correct. range(4) repeats the helper function four times.
failureMessage: Use the exact number of repeats you want. Four repeats means range(4).

title: Repair the loop body
question: Which replacement fixes the code so the helper is repeated by the loop instead of running only once after it?
boardHint: The repeated action must be indented inside the loop body.
unlockConnection: Loop repairs check whether the combo feeder is being charged by real repeated logic, not by one final call outside the loop.
successMessage: Correct. follow_bonus() belongs inside the loop body so it repeats three times.
failureMessage: The loop body must contain the repeated helper call, and it has to stay indented under the for-line.
